title: Linux Permissions Deep Dive
description: Go beyond basic chmod with symbolic permissions, ownership, umask, ACLs, and special permission bits like SUID, SGID, and sticky bit.

tasks.prompt: Add execute permission for the group on 'deploy.sh' using symbolic (letter) notation.
tasks.hint: Use chmod with group add execute: 'chmod g+x deploy.sh'
tasks.explanation: Symbolic chmod uses who (u/g/o/a), operator (+/-/=), and permissions (r/w/x). 'g+x' adds execute for the group.

tasks.prompt: Remove write permission for others on 'secret.conf' using symbolic notation.
tasks.hint: Use chmod with others remove write: 'chmod o-w secret.conf'
tasks.explanation: 'o-w' removes write permission from the 'others' category, preventing non-owner/non-group users from modifying the file.

tasks.prompt: Set permissions on 'database.conf' to owner read/write, group read, others none (octal 640).
tasks.explanation: Octal 640 means owner gets rw- (6), group gets r-- (4), and others get --- (0).

tasks.prompt: Change only the owner of 'app.log' to user 'www-data' (leave group unchanged).
tasks.hint: Use chown with just the username: 'chown www-data app.log'
tasks.explanation: When only a username is specified (no colon), 'chown' changes the owner while preserving the existing group.

tasks.prompt: Change the group of 'shared_dir' to 'developers' recursively.
tasks.hint: Use chgrp with recursive flag (-R): 'chgrp -R developers shared_dir'
tasks.explanation: 'chgrp' changes group ownership. '-R' applies the change to all files and subdirectories within the target.

tasks.prompt: Display the current umask value in octal format.
tasks.hint: Run the umask command with no arguments: 'umask'
tasks.explanation: 'umask' shows the default permission mask subtracted from new files and directories at creation time.

tasks.prompt: Grant user 'alice' read and write permissions on 'project.txt' using an ACL.
tasks.explanation: 'setfacl -m' modifies ACL entries. 'u:alice:rw' grants read and write to the specific user alice.

tasks.prompt: Display the ACL entries and standard permissions for 'project.txt'.
tasks.explanation: 'getfacl' shows both traditional Unix permissions and any extended ACL entries on a file.

tasks.prompt: Set the SUID bit on '/usr/local/bin/backup_tool' using octal mode 4755.
tasks.explanation: The leading 4 in octal 4755 sets the SUID bit, causing the program to run with the file owner's privileges.

tasks.prompt: Set the sticky bit on the directory '/tmp/shared' using octal mode 1777.
tasks.explanation: The sticky bit (1xxx) on a directory ensures users can only delete files they own, even in world-writable directories.
